title: Pure Storage FlashBlade S3 bucket capacity and request rate anomalies
dataSources: Object store metrics on FlashBlade indexed as `purestorage:array` or dedicated object sourcetype from Unified Add-on
app: Pure Storage Unified Add-on for Splunk (5513)
spl:
index=storage sourcetype="purestorage:array"
| search s3 OR object OR bucket
| eval bucket=coalesce(bucket_name, s3_bucket)
| eval bytes=coalesce(bucket_bytes_used, object_bytes, logical_bytes)
| eval ops=coalesce(s3_ops_per_sec, object_ops, ops_ps)
| timechart span=1h sum(bytes) as bytes latest(ops) as ops by bucket
cimSpl:
| tstats `summariesonly` max(Performance.storage_used_percent) as used_pct
  from datamodel=Performance where nodename=Performance.Storage
  by Performance.host Performance.object span=1h
| where used_pct > 80
| sort - used_pct
dataModelAcceleration: Enable Data Model Acceleration for the CIM Performance data model (Performance.Storage node / storage_used_percent) after vendor fields are aliased to CIM; otherwise the cimSpl tstats variant may return no rows.
description: Bucket-level growth and request spikes indicate data leaks, misconfigured sync jobs, or abusive clients before capacity or CPU limits trip.
value: Protects object storage SLAs for modern apps and data lakes on FlashBlade.
implementation: Map Purity//FB REST object counters; if bucket metrics are coarse, aggregate by account with `stats` and alert on week-over-week growth >30%.
visualization: Line chart (bytes and ops), table (bucket, growth%).

references.title: Pure Storage Unified Add-on for Splunk (Splunkbase)

knownFalsePositives: Legitimate bulk operations, month-end reporting, or new application releases can change access patterns; compare to change windows and owner notifications.
grandmaExplanation: We watch how full your storage is getting and give you time to add space or clean up snapshots and old data before an application or job suddenly stops working.
